root_text.an4.63:1.1: “Sabrahmakāni, bhikkhave, tāni kulāni yesaṁ puttānaṁ mātāpitaro ajjhāgāre pūjitā honti.
root_text.an4.63:1.2: Sapubbācariyakāni, bhikkhave, tāni kulāni, yesaṁ puttānaṁ mātāpitaro ajjhāgāre pūjitā honti.
root_text.an4.63:1.3: Sapubbadevatāni, bhikkhave, tāni kulāni yesaṁ puttānaṁ mātāpitaro ajjhāgāre pūjitā honti.
root_text.an4.63:1.4: Sāhuneyyakāni, bhikkhave, tāni kulāni yesaṁ puttānaṁ mātāpitaro ajjhāgāre pūjitā honti.
root_text.an4.63:2.6: Bahukārā, bhikkhave, mātāpitaro, puttānaṁ āpādakā posakā imassa lokassa dassetāroti.

translation_text.an4.63:1.1: “Mendicants, a family where the children honor their parents in their home is said to live with divinity.
translation_text.an4.63:1.2: A family where the children honor their parents in their home is said to live with the first tutors.
translation_text.an4.63:1.3: A family where the children honor their parents in their home is said to live with the elder gods.
translation_text.an4.63:1.4: A family where the children honor their parents in their home is said to live with those worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods.
translation_text.an4.63:2.1: ‘Divinity’ is a term for your parents.
translation_text.an4.63:2.2: ‘First tutors’ is a term for your parents.
translation_text.an4.63:2.3: ‘Elder gods’ is a term for your parents.
translation_text.an4.63:2.4: ‘Worthy of an offering dedicated to the gods’ is a term for your parents.
translation_text.an4.63:2.6: Parents are very helpful to their children, they raise them, nurture them, and show them the world.
translation_text.an4.63:3.3: They’re worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods <j>from their children,
translation_text.an4.63:4.2: would revere them and honor them
translation_text.an4.63:5.3: they’re praised in this life by the astute,
translation_text.an4.63:5.4: and they depart to rejoice in heaven.”
